purpose: LLM-as-verifier authorship surface — one ADVISORY stylometric-consistency signal over a query/reference text pair (Huang et al. 2024 "Can LLMs Identify Authorship?", InstructAV, CAVE). A model-based SECOND OPINION beside the stylometric general_imposters harness: a pluggable LLM judge reads the two texts as prose and emits a 5-band ordinal band (consistent / leans_consistent / cannot_determine / leans_inconsistent / inconsistent) plus a CAVE-style decomposed per-feature rationale with verbatim span pointers. Descriptive, no verdict: the band vocabulary contains NO same-author / different-author / AI / human token, there is no probability / score / confidence numeric, and the result ships uncalibrated, bound to a named judge model + prompt fingerprint. Never a held-out validator, a voicewright selection / fitness signal, a reward, or a training target. `cannot_determine` is the designed gray-zone outcome, not an error. The mock / manifest judges are stdlib (M1); the anthropic / openai / gemini API kinds read the prose (M2, lazy SDK).
use_when: You want a model-based second opinion on whether two texts read as stylometrically consistent, to corroborate or contrast against general_imposters / voice_distance
You have a query text and a register-comparable reference text (authorship verification is pairwise)
You can accept a refusal — cannot_determine is a designed outcome, and the band is uncalibrated and judge+prompt-relative
do_not_use_when: You need an authorship verdict (same-author / different-author), an AI-vs-human determination, or a probability / score — the surface refuses all of them
You have only one text — this is a pairwise AV second opinion, not an "is this AI" classifier
You would wire the band into a selection / held-out / fitness / reward / training target — anti-Goodhart, it is read-only advisory context for the human

compute.cost_note: mock / manifest are free + stdlib (M1); the anthropic / openai / gemini API kinds cost per the provider (one pairwise comparison per call, M2)

examples.description: Model-free CI / dry-run via the deterministic mock judge
Consume a pre-computed VerifierResult from an out-of-band judge
